A car left Canterbury at 7:12 am and arrived in Birmingham, 180 miles distant at 10:57 am. What is the average speed in miles per hour?

  1. 42
  2. 44
  3. 46
  4. 48
  5. 50


Quantitative Aptitude: A car left Canterbury at 7.12 am and arrived in Birmingham, 180 miles distant at 10.57 am.

  2. 3 n
  3. 2 n
  4. n 2
    5.

An aircraft files 930 miles in 75 minutes. How many miles does it fly in 4 hours 45 minutes assuming a constant speed?

  1. 3112
  2. 3477
  3. 3512
  4. 3522
  5. 3534

If a  + b + c = 0, where \( a \neq b \neq c \), then what is the value of:

\( \frac{a^{2}}{2a^{2}+bc}+\frac{b^2}{2b^{2}+ac}+\frac{c^{2}}{2c^{2}+ab} \)

A water tank has three taps A, B, and C. A fills four buckets in 24 mm, B fills 8 buckets in 1 hour and C fills 2 buckets in 20 minutes. If all the taps are opened together a full tank is emptied in 2 hours. If a bucket can hold 5 litres of water, what is the capacity of the tank?

Along a road lie an odd number of stones placed at intervals of 10 m. These stones have to be assembled around the middle stone. A person can carry only one stone at a time. A man carried out the job starting with the stone in the middle, carrying stones in succession, thereby covering a distance of 4.8 km. Then the number of stones is:

\( A(x, y) + x + y \)

A, S, M and Dare functions of x and y, and they are defined as follows:

\( A(x, y) + x + y \) 

\( S(x, y) = x - y \)

\( M(x, y) = xy \)

where \( y \neq 0 \).

Which of the following value of x do not satisfy the inequality \( x^{2}-3x+2>0 \) at all?

In a locality, there are five small towns, \( A, B, C, D \) and \( E \). The distances of these towns from each other are as follows:

\( AB=2km AC=2km AD>2km AE>3km BC=2km \) 

\( BD=4km BE=3km CD=2km CE=3km DE>3km \).

If a ration shop is to be set up within \( 2 \) km of each city, how many ration shops will be required?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1996 | Question: 123

In a locality, there are five small towns, \( A, B, C, D \) and \( E \). The distances of these towns from each other are as follows:

\( AB=2km AC=2km AD>2km AE>3km BC=2km \) 

\( BD=4km BE=3km CD=2km CE=3km DE>3km \).

If a ration shop is to be set up within \( 3 \) km of each city, how many ratio shops will be required?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1996 | Question: 124

A cube of side \( 12 \) cm is painted red on all the faces and then cut into smaller cubes, each of side \( 3 \) cm. What is the total number of smaller cubes having none of their faces painted?

The points of intersection of three lines, \( 2X + 3Y - 5 = 0, 5X - 7Y + 2 = 0 \), and \( 9X-5Y-4 =0 \):

are on lines parallel to each other.

Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1996 | Question: 134

In a locality, two-thirds of the people have cable-TV, one-fifth have VCR, and one-tenth have both, what is the fraction of people having either cable TV or VCR?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1996 | Question: 135

If ABCD is a square and BCE is an equilateral triangle, what is the measure of the angle DEC?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1996 | Question: 136

I bought \( 5 \) pens, \( 7 \) pencils and \( 4 \) erasers. Rajan bought \( 6 \) pens, \( 8 \) erasers and \( 14 \) pencils for an amount which was half more than what I had paid. What percent of the total amount paid by me was paid for the pens?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1996 | Question: 137

If x is a positive integer such that. \( 2x + 12 \) is perfectly divisibly by \( x \), then the number of possible values of \( x \) is:



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1996 | Question: 138

A watch dealer incurs an expense of Rs \( 150 \) for producing every watch. He also incurs an additional expenditure of Rs. \( 30,000 \), which is independent of the number of watches produced. If he is able to sell a watch during the season, he sells it for Rs. \( 250 \). If he fails to do so, he has to sell each watch for Rs. \( 100 \).

If he produces \( 1500 \) watches, what is the number of watches that he must sell during the season in order to break even, given that he is able to sell all the watches produced?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1996 | Question: 140

In a watch, the minute hand crosses the hour hand for the third time exactly after every 3 hrs 18 mm 15 seconds of watch time. What is the time gained or lost by this watch in one day?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1996 | Question: 144

A race consists of three stretches \( A, B \) and \( C \) of \( 2 \)kms length each. The mode of coverage, and maximum and minimum of speeds possible in each stretch are:

Stretch Mode Minimum Speed (Km/h) Maximum Speed (Km/h)
A Car 40 60
B Motorcycle 30 50
C Bicycle 10 20

Speed in a given stretch remains constant. The previous record was ten minutes to complete the race.

Anshuman travelled at the minimum speed by car and at maximum speed over the stretch \( B \). What should be his minimum speed over stretch \( C \) to beat the previous record?

 



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 101
options: Not possible to beat the previous record

questions.question:
A race consists of three stretches \( A, B \) and \( C \) of \( 2 \)kms length each. The mode of coverage, and maximum and minimum of speeds possible in each stretch are:

Stretch Mode Minimum Speed (Km/h) Maximum Speed (Km/h)
A Car 40 60
B Motorcycle 30 50
C Bicycle 10 20

Speed in a given stretch remains constant. The previous record was ten minutes to complete the race.

Mr. Hare travelled at the slowest speed in stretch \( A \) and took the same amount of time to travel in stretch \( B \) as he took in stretch \( A \). If he took fifty percent more time than the previous record to complete the race, what was his speed in the stretch \( C \)?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 102

A race consists of three stretches \( A, B \) and \( C \) of \( 2 \)kms length each. The mode of coverage, and maximum and minimum of speeds possible in each stretch are:

Stretch Mode Minimum Speed (Km/h) Maximum Speed (Km/h)
A Car 40 60
B Motorcycle 30 50
C Bicycle 10 20

Speed in a given stretch remains constant. The previous record was ten minutes to complete the race.

Mr. Tortoise travelled in the overall race at an average speed of \( 20 \) km/h. However, his average speed over the first two stretches combined was four times that over the last stretch. What was his speed over the last stretch?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 103

There are \( 60 \) students in a class. These students are divided into three groups \( A, B \) and \( C \) of \( 15, 20 \) and \( 25 \) students each. The groups \( A \) and \( C \) are combined to form group \( D \).

What is the average weight of the students in group D?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 104
options: More than the average weight of A
More than the average weight of C
Less than the average weight of C

questions.question:
There are \( 60 \) students in a class. These students are divided into three groups \( A, B \) and \( C \) of \( 15, 20 \) and \( 25 \) students each. The groups \( A \) and \( C \) are combined to form group \( D \).

If one students from Group A is shifted to group B, which of the following will be true?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 105
options: The average weight of both groups increases.
The average weight of both the groups decreases.
The average weight of the class remains the same.

questions.question:
There are \( 60 \) students in a class. These students are divided into three groups \( A, B \) and \( C \) of \( 15, 20 \) and \( 25 \) students each. The groups \( A \) and \( C \) are combined to form group \( D \).

If all the students of the class have the same weight, then which of the following is false?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 106
options: The average weight of all the four groups is the same.
The total weight of A and C is twice the total weight of \( B \).
The average weight of D is greater than the average weight of \( A \).
The average weight of all the groups remains the same even if a number of students are shifted from one group to another.

questions.question:

If \( \log_{2}[ \log_{7} (x^{2}-x+37)]=1 \) then what could be the value of \( x \)?

How many values of the natural number \( n \) are possible so that the expression \( (16n^{2}+7n+6)/n \) is also a natural number?

A dry fruit seller purchased 3 kinds of nuts at the rate of Rs. \( 100 \)/kg, Rs. \( 80 \)/kg and Rs. \( 60 \)/kg. He then mixed them, respectively, in the ratio \( 3:4:5 \) by weight and sold the same to a customer at \( 50 \)% profit. The price at which he sold to the customer is



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 112

Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 118

The sum of the areas of two circles which touch each other externally is \( 153\pi \). If the sum of their radii is \( 15 \), find the ratio of the larger to the smaller radius.



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 119

If \( m \) and \( n \) are integers divisible by \( 5 \), which of the following is not necessarily true?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 120

Which of the following is true?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 121

A survey of 200 people in a community who watched at least one of the three channels – BBC, CNN and DD showed that \( 80 \)% of the people watched DD, \( 22 \)% watched BBC, and \( 15 \)% watched CNN.

What is the maximum percent of people who can watch all the three channels?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 122

A survey of \( 200 \) people in a community who watched at least one of the three channels – BBC, CNN and DD showed that \( 80 \)% of the people watched DD, \( 22 \)% watched BBC, and \( 15 \)% watched CNN.

If \( 5 \)% of the people watched DD and CNN, \( 10 \)% watched DD and BBC, then what percent of the people watched BBC and CNN only?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 123

A survey of \( 200 \) people in a community who watched at least one of the three channels – BBC, CNN and DD showed that \( 80 \)% of the people watched DD, \( 22 \)% watched BBC, and \( 15 \)% watched CNN.

Referring to the previous question, how many percents of the people watched all the three channels?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 124

A man earns \( x \)% on the first \( 2000 \) rupees and \( y \)% on the rest of his income. If he earns Rs \( 700 \) from Rs \( 4000 \) and Rs \( 900 \) from Rs \( 5000 \) of income, find \( x \).



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 125

AB is the diameter of the given circle, while points C and D lie on the circumference as shown. If AB is \( 15 \) cm, AC is \( 12 \) cm and BD is \( 9 \) cm, find the area of the quadrilateral ACBD.



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 126

P, Q and R are three consecutive odd numbers in ascending order. If the value of three times P is three less than two times R, find the value of R.



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 127

For these questions, the following functions have been defined:

\( la(x,y,z) = min(x+y, y+z) \)

\( le (x,y,z) = max(x–y, y–z) \)

\( ma(x,y,z)=(1/2) [le(x,y,z)+la(x,y,z)] \)

Given that \( x > y > z > 0 \), which of the following is necessarily true?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 128

For these questions, the following functions have been defined:

\( la(x,y,z) = min(x+y, y+z) \)

\( le (x,y,z) = max(x–y, y–z) \)

\( ma(x,y,z)=(1/2) [le(x,y,z)+la(x,y,z)] \)

What is the value of \( ma(10, 4, le(la(10, 5, 3), 5,3)) \)

For these questions, the following functions have been defined:

\( la(x,y,z) = min(x+y, y+z) \) 

\( le (x,y,z) = max(x–y, y–z) \) 

\( ma(x,y,z)=(1/2) [le(x,y,z)+la(x,y,z)] \)

For \( x = 15, y = 10 \) and \( z = 9 \), find the value of: \( le (x, \min(y, x–z), le(9, 8, ma(x,y,z)) \).



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 130

\( ABC \) is a three-digit number in which \( A>O \). The value of \( ABC \) is equal to the sum of the factorials of its three digits. What is the value of \( B \)?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 131

The adjoining figure shows a set of concentric squares.

If the diagonal of the innermost square is \( 2 \) units, and if the distance between the corresponding corners of any two successive squares is \( 1 \) unit, find the difference between the areas of the eighth and the seventh square, counting from the innermost square.



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 132

\( A, B \) and \( C \) are defined as follows:

\( A=(2.000004)\div [(2.000004)^{2}+(4.000008)] \)

\( B=(3.000003)\div [(3.000003)^{2}+(9.000009)] \)

\( C=(4.000002)\div [(4.000002)^{2}+(8.000004)] \)

Which of the following is true about the value of the above three expressions?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 133

In a triangle \( ABC \), points \( P, Q \) and \( R \) are the mid-points of the sides \( AB, BC \) and \( CA \) respectively. If the area of the triangle \( ABC \) is \( 20 \) sq units, find the area of the triangle \( PQR \) 



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 135

In a rectangle, the difference between the sum of the adjacent sides and the diagonal is half the length of the longer side. What is the ratio of the shorter to the longer side?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 136

Boston is \( 4 \) hours ahead of Frankfurt and two hours behind India. X leaves Frankfurt at \( 06:00 \) p.m. on Friday and reaches Boston the next day. After waiting there for two hours, he leaves exactly at noon and reaches India at \( 01:00 \) a.m. On his return journey, he takes the same route as before, but halts at Boston for one hour less than his previous halt there. He then proceeds to Frankfurt.

If his journey, including stoppages, was covered at an average speed of \( 180 \) miles per hour, what was the distance between Frankfurt and India?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 139

Boston is \( 4 \) hours ahead of Frankfurt and two hours behind India. X leaves Frankfurt at \( 06:00 \) p.m. on Friday and reaches Boston the next day. After waiting there for two hours, he leaves exactly at noon and reaches India at \( 01:00 \) a.m. On his return journey, he takes the same route as before, but halts at Boston for one hour less than his previous halt there. He then proceeds to Frankfurt.

If \( X \) had started his return journey from India at \( 02:55 \) a.m. on the same day that he reached there, after how much time would he reach Frankfurt?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 140

Boston is \( 4 \) hours ahead of Frankfurt and two hours behind India. X leaves Frankfurt at \( 06:00 \) p.m. on Friday and reaches Boston the next day. After waiting there for two hours, he leaves exactly at noon and reaches India at \( 01:00 \) a.m. On his return journey, he takes the same route as before, but halts at Boston for one hour less than his previous halt there. He then proceeds to Frankfurt.

What was X’s average speed for the entire journey?

Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 142

In the given figure, \( EADF \) is a rectangle and \( ABC \) is a triangle whose vertices lie on the sides of \( EADF \). \( AE = 22,BE = 6,CF = l6 \) and \( BF = 2 \). Find the length of the line joining the midpoints of the sides \( AB \) and \( BC \).



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 143

These questions are based on the following data:

A thief flees City \( A \) in a car towards City \( B \) on a stretch of straight road, \( 300 \) kilometres long, at the speed of \( 60 \) km/hr. In \( 15 \) minutes a police party (X) leaves city \( A \) to chase the thief at \( 65 \) km/hr.

After leaving how long does it take for the police party \( X \) to catch the thief’?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 144

These questions are based on the following data:

A thief flees City \( A \) in a car towards City \( B \) on a stretch of straight road, \( 300 \) kilometres long, at the speed of \( 60 \) km/hr. In \( 15 \) minutes a police party (X) leaves city \( A \) to chase the thief at \( 65 \) km/hr.

If a police party \( (Y) \) were to leave City \( B \) at the same time as police party \( X \) leaving City \( A \) to catch the thief at 60km/hr., then which of the following statements is true?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 145
options: Party \( X \) reaches \( 37.5 \) minutes after Party \( Y \) has caught the thief.
Party \( Y \) reaches \( 37.5 \) minutes after Party \( X \) has caught the thief
Party \( X \) and Party \( Y \) has caught the thief together.
Party \( X \) was \( 20 \) km away when the thief caught.

questions.question:
The following table gives the quantity of Apples (in tonnes) arriving in the Agricultural Produce Market Committee (APMC), New Delhi in the year 1990-91. In the months in which demand was more than supply, the additional demand was met by the stock from cold storage.

Month HP UP J&K Cold Total
April 7 0 7 59 73
May 12 1 0 0 13
June 9741 257 8017 0 18015
July 71497 10 18750 0 90257
Aug 77675 0 20286 0 97961
Sep. 53912 0 5660 0 110514
Oct. 12604 0 79591 24 92219
Nov. 3499 0 41872 42 45413
Dec. 1741 0 14822 15 16578
Jan 315 0 10922 201 11438
Feb. 25 0 11183 77 11285
March 0 0 683 86 760

What was the maximum percentage of Apples supplied by any state in any of the months?
